differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Organized into predefined tables with fixed schemas.

differences.title: 2. SCHEMA
differences.leftDesc: Schema must be defined before data insertion.
differences.rightDesc: Schema can evolve easily, no predefined structure.

differences.title: 3. QUERY LANGUAGE
differences.leftDesc: Standardized, powerful SQL for complex queries and joins.
differences.rightDesc: JSON-based queries, optimized for document retrieval.

differences.title: 4. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Primarily scales by increasing server resources (CPU, RAM).
differences.rightDesc: Scales out by adding more servers to the cluster.

differences.title: 5. ACID COMPLIANCE
differences.leftDesc: Ensures Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability for transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Offers ACID compliance but often relaxed for performance.

differences.title: 6. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for financial systems, CRM, and applications with complex relationships.
differences.rightDesc: Best for content management, catalogs, and rapidly changing data.

differences.title: 7. DATA INTEGRITY
differences.leftDesc: Enforces data integrity through constraints and relationships.
differences.rightDesc: Integrity managed more at the application level.
